Also tracked as BlueRabbit and previously attributed to an Iran-based actor, it makes destruction an optional post-compromise command, so defenders should hunt for persistent backdoor activity rather than waiting for wiper detonation.

title: NCSC issues warning over Russian intelligence-backed threat group
blurb: The NCSC, alongside 18 partner agencies, warned that FSB Centre 16 (aka Berserk Bear, Static Tundra, Ghost Blizzard) is opportunistically compromising internet-facing routers and critical infrastructure via SNMP scans and Cisco Smart Install flaws, targeting communications, energy, healthcare, defense, and financial-services organizations. Defenders should disable Cisco SMI, restrict SNMP exposure, and patch affected network devices, as the UK concurrently sanctioned 24 individuals and entities tied to the operations.

title: GNSS Spoofing Detection in TDD Networks: A 3GPP Standards-Based Security Framework
blurb: This paper proposes a framework for detecting GNSS spoofing against TDD mobile networks, where spoofed timing on GNSS-disciplined grandmaster clocks can corrupt synchronization across downstream base stations. It works within existing 3GPP management structures, adding timing alarms and performance counters (TS 28.111, TS 28.552), topology-aware correlation that groups gNB-DUs by serving grandmaster, and a bridge to SECHAND incident handling. Monte Carlo simulation shows over 95% detection for drift above 0.5 ns/s with under 1% false positives.

title: An Explainable Agentic System for Detection of Conversational Scams with Summary-Based Memory
blurb: This paper introduces an explainable agentic system for detecting conversational scams, the slow-build "pig butchering" and romance frauds that span weeks and evade single-message phishing detectors. Using summary-based memory to track a conversation over time, it flags all 83 scams in the LoveFraud02 corpus and hits 97.8% accuracy on the authors' new ConScamBench-278 benchmark spanning eight scam types, which they released for reproducible evaluation.

title: EU sanctions Russian GRU military hackers over cyberattacks
blurb: The EU and UK jointly sanctioned dozens of Russian individuals and entities, including GRU intelligence officers and cybercriminals, and publicly attributed control of several threat groups, including Turla, to the FSB's 16th Centre. The package targets operators behind years of cyberespionage against government and critical-infrastructure networks across Europe, alongside individuals tied to the Lumma Stealer operation and Rybar disinformation efforts.

title: Elon Musk says SpaceXAI will delete all Grok Build user data following privacy concerns
blurb: Security researchers found that SpaceXAI's Grok Build uploaded entire Git repositories to Google Cloud, exposing private source code, full commit histories, and hardcoded API keys. Elon Musk said the company will delete all affected user data, though exposed API keys should be treated as compromised and rotated regardless of deletion.

title: LLM-Guided Program Evolution for Targeted Black-Box Attacks on Perceptual Hash Algorithms
blurb: This paper introduces an evolutionary framework using LLM-guided program evolution to mount targeted black-box second-image attacks on perceptual hash algorithms, forging images whose hashes collide with a chosen target. Tested against pHash, PDQ, PhotoDNA, and NeuralHash, it matches or beats existing baselines with fewer queries and lower L2 distortion, cutting the composite attack score by 41.2% on NeuralHash and 38.3% on PDQ, which exposes collision risks in deployed content-moderation pipelines.

title: FlowArk: Boosting Agentic Data-flow Analysis for Android Apps via Context-Aware Knowledge Reuse
blurb: FlowArk is a knowledge-reuse system that cuts the cost of batch agentic data-flow analysis on Android apps by distilling completed analyses into matchable knowledge entries and injecting them into later, context-isolated agent instances. Evaluated on 4,685 source-to-sink tasks across 50 open-source apps atop OpenCode, it held analysis quality steady while cutting end-to-end API cost 26.83%, and completed 36.66% more tasks under a fixed $100 budget (1,060 vs. 776).

title: Understanding the Impact of AI Code Assistants on Security API Usage: An Empirical Study
blurb: A study of 44 professional developers found that GitHub Copilot improves functional correctness and marginally reduces some insecure patterns, but does not significantly improve secure use of security APIs. Developers rarely raised security concerns while working with Copilot, and many failed to recognize that their final implementations remained insecure. The authors recommend pairing AI assistants with dedicated security review, since correct-looking output masks vulnerable API usage that developers won't catch on their own.

title: AMT-X: Phase-Structured Multi-Turn Red-Teaming with Checklist-Gated Evaluation
blurb: AMT-X is a multi-turn red-teaming framework that models LLM jailbreaks as a reproducible state machine driven by the victim model's own semantic signals, and scores success with a multi-role jury whose phase-conditioned checklists gate on actionable harm rather than a single judge. Across six frontier models under default alignment, it hit 97.6-100% attack success under a lenient threshold but 66.7-78.6% when requiring complete, operational detail. The 33-point gap argues for scoring evals on operational completeness, not binary refusal.

title: Google Credential Provider for Windows (GCPW) now supports FIDO2-compliant physical security keys as a second factor for authentication
blurb: Google updated Google Credential Provider for Windows (GCPW) to support FIDO2-compliant physical security keys as a second factor, letting admins enforce hardware-key 2SV at the Windows login screen. The feature also accepts passkeys from nearby Bluetooth-connected mobile devices, is available to all Workspace customers, and began a gradual rollout on July 13 with no end-user configuration required.

title: Governments to enterprises: Improve your router security hygiene
blurb: A multinational advisory from 19 agencies across North America, the UK, Europe, and Australia warns that Russian state-sponsored actors are opportunistically compromising critical infrastructure by scanning for routers running SNMPv1/v2 with default community strings, then exfiltrating config files containing plaintext credentials. The actors are also exploiting Cisco Smart Install flaws CVE-2018-0171 and CVE-2008-4128 for unauthenticated RCE, so disable SMI, retire legacy SNMP, and rotate default strings.

title: Automated Stealthy Wear-Out Attack on Digital Twins With Deep Reinforcement Learning
blurb: This paper introduces a stealthy wear-out attack that uses Deep Reinforcement Learning to covertly manipulate control signals in Digital Twin-enabled industrial systems, driving up torque on a targeted robotic joint to accelerate physical degradation while evading anomaly detection. Tested against a UR10e robotic arm, the SAC-based adversary raised joint wear and maintenance costs undetected, showing that DT integration expands the ICS attack surface to physical sabotage that current detectors miss.

title: UK charges suspects linked to Russian Coms call spoofing platform
blurb: UK authorities charged five London residents following an NCA investigation into Russian Coms, a caller ID spoofing platform used to place over 1.8 million scam calls that impersonated banks, telecoms, and law enforcement. The service, active from 2020 until its March 2024 takedown, has been linked to roughly $12M in losses across an estimated 170,000 victims, with defendants due at Westminster Magistrates' Court on August 14.

title: Progress Prompts ShareFile Storage Zone Controller Shutdown Amid Security Concerns
blurb: Progress Software told ShareFile customers to manually shut down their Storage Zone Controllers after detecting a "credible external security threat" targeting the on-premises storage component, and has temporarily disabled ShareFile account access as it investigates. Users suspect exploitation of CVE-2026-2699 (CVSS 9.8) and CVE-2026-2701 (CVSS 9.1), two March-patched flaws that chain to unauthenticated RCE, so unpatched controllers should stay offline until Progress confirms a fix.

title: New tutorials on underground hacking forums have roughly doubled
blurb: Radware analyzed 8,870 tutorial posts across 24 deep- and dark-web forums, finding that new hacking guides roughly doubled from ~45 per month in 2024 to 110-140 in 2026. Carding overtook black-hat SEO as the top category, rising to 38% of tutorials, and many guides now pair account-takeover techniques with cash-out methods to hand readers a complete fraud playbook. Telecom and social media were the most-referenced targets, reflecting their role in OTP interception and verified-account abuse.

title: Zimbra Patches Critical Code Execution Vulnerability
blurb: Zimbra patched a critical stored XSS in the Classic Web Client that enables zero-click code execution when a victim opens a specially crafted email, exposing mailbox contents, session data, and account settings. The flaw was reported by Google TAG, which typically tracks state-sponsored and commercial-spyware activity, and is fixed in ZCS 10.1.19; no CVE has been assigned yet, so patch to 10.1.19 immediately.

title: Cross-Layer Misalignment Detection in Agent Skills: A Progressive Loading-Aware Contrastive Learning Approach
blurb: This paper introduces PL-HCL, a contrastive-learning framework that detects "cross-layer misalignment" in LLM Agent Skills, where a skill's metadata description doesn't match its actual instructions or execution-time behavior. Trained on 264,000 open-source skills plus a human-verified challenge set, it lifts Macro-F1 from roughly 0.45 for unadapted baselines to 0.87-0.89 across backbones. For teams pulling third-party skills from open marketplaces, it offers a screening tool to flag deceptive or inconsistent skills before agents load them.

title: New MemGhost Attack Plants Persistent False Memories in AI Agents Through One Email
blurb: Researchers disclosed MemGhost, a stealth memory-injection attack that plants persistent false facts in personal AI agents like the open-source OpenClaw via a single inbound email, using the agent's own file tools to silently rewrite its MEMORY.md and steer later sessions. Detailed in the arXiv paper "When Claws Remember but Do Not Tell" (6 July 2026), the technique evades notice because agents hide file edits, users rarely inspect raw memory, and scheduled background runs surface no output.

title: Closing the Loop: An Access-Control Architecture for Automated, Anomaly-Driven Network Revocation in IoT Deployments
blurb: This paper presents an access-control architecture that automates enforcement after IoT anomaly detection, using only deployed protocols: 802.1X with EAP-TLS for device auth and a RADIUS server as a continuous policy decision point that evicts active sessions via Change-of-Authorization Disconnect-Request and permanently excludes devices through certificate revocation. A one-class detector fusing cluster, volumetric, and protocol-signature scores hit 0.9964 AUC across 24 attack scenarios, triggering device eviction in 335.8ms and certificate revocation in a further 111.5ms.

title: Turning the Tables on Email Scammers With 'ScamBuster'
blurb: Filigran principal engineer Laurent Giovannoni released ScamBuster, an open-source, AI-driven system that engages phishing scammers with victim personas to collect intelligence on attacker infrastructure, financial details, and related campaigns. The inbound-only system, in production since November 2025 and set for a Black Hat USA 2026 reveal, never initiates contact and answers only incoming scam emails to gather data for defenders and law enforcement.

title: Cloudflare launches Precursor to catch bots by watching entire sessions
blurb: Cloudflare made Precursor generally available, a bot detection system that scores mouse movement, scroll rhythm, typing cadence, and tab visibility across an entire browsing session rather than at a single arrival challenge. The continuous Bot Score follows visitors through a site and flags internal contradictions like pointer activity while a page is hidden, closing the page-reload evasion that resets per-request CAPTCHA checks.

title: PromptGraph: Graph-Guided Prompt Sanitization for Balancing Privacy and Utility in LLM Inference
blurb: PromptGraph is a prompt-sanitization method that models pairwise relationships between text spans, not just individual identifiers, to counter contextual privacy leakage in LLM inference. It represents each prompt as an attributed graph, scoring span-level privacy risk on nodes and utility-preserving dependencies on edges, then selects a protected span set that maximizes privacy gain while penalizing lost context. Sanitized spans are masked locally and restored only after consistency checks, which the authors report beats prior baselines on the privacy-utility tradeoff.

title: Can Watermarking Techniques Help Prevent LLM Model Stealing?
blurb: This paper proposes defenses against model-stealing attacks that extract hidden-layer dimensions and other parameters from black-box commercial LLMs, building on Carlini et al.'s 2024 extraction technique. The authors perturb the logits layer using watermarking-inspired methods and show empirically that the defense blocks extraction while limiting model-quality degradation across configurations. For teams exposing production models via API, it argues logit-layer noise is a viable mitigation without sacrificing utility.

title: European "age verification" "app" forcing everyone to use Android or iOS
blurb: The EU's age-verification app reference implementation requires Google Play Integrity attestation, meaning devices must run a Google- or Apple-approved OS build to pass verification and excluding de-Googled Android, custom ROMs, and Linux phones. A GitHub discussion pushing back on the requirement has drawn nearly 600 comments arguing the mandate hands two US platform vendors gatekeeping power over access to EU digital identity.

title: 'Yellow Teams' Are Defining the Future of AI Security
blurb: Yellow teams" — build teams that sit between red and blue — are emerging as the group defining how organizations will attack and defend with frontier AI. Sparked by Anthropic's Project Glasswing (previewing Claude Mythos) and OpenAI's Daybreak (GPT 5.5), these engineers build both the exploitation frameworks red teams wield and the mitigations blue teams lack from vendors. Early users note Mythos hedges findings and flags context-free vulnerabilities, so human validation remains essential.

title: Key findings from the 2026 Public Sector M-Trends report and beyond
blurb: Mandiant's 2026 Public Sector M-Trends report, drawn from over 500,000 hours of 2025 incident response, clocks the median time from initial-access-broker foothold to ransomware hand-off at 22 seconds, outpacing any human triage workflow. Public sector defenders should prioritize automated containment, extend telemetry retention past the standard 90 days to catch multi-year espionage persistence, and monitor non-human identities like OAuth tokens and service accounts, which are increasingly the pivot point for SaaS-integration breach chains.

title: World Cup grudge attackers may have scored Argentine FA access via year-old infostealer infection
blurb: Researchers linked a compromise of the Argentine Football Association to credentials stolen by an infostealer that infected an employee device roughly a year earlier and were never rotated. The case shows how stale infostealer logs sold on criminal markets keep granting access long after the initial infection, so defenders should force credential resets for any account exposed in known stealer dumps rather than assuming old infections are contained.

title: Forget typosquatting; slopsquatting is the software supply chain threat created by AI coding tools
blurb: Slopsquatting is a supply-chain attack that weaponizes LLM hallucinations: AI coding assistants invent plausible-sounding package names, attackers register those names on public registries and populate them with malware. Because these are novel invented names rather than misspellings of real libraries, registry typosquatting defenses like blocking "crossenv" against "cross-env" don't catch them. Defenders should pin and vet AI-suggested dependencies before install, and validate that every package an assistant recommends actually exists and is legitimately maintained.

title: LAPD lets contract with surveillance giant Flock expire, citing ‘serious concerns’ over civil liberties and privacy
blurb: The LAPD, the third-largest US police department and one of Flock Safety's largest government customers, is letting its three-year contract with the license-plate-tracking firm expire on Saturday, citing "serious concerns" over civil liberties, privacy, and data collection. The move follows similar cancellations by Mountain View, California and South Portland, Maine over fears that federal immigration officials used Flock's 80,000-camera network to track people in violation of sanctuary-city policies.

title: German firm files for insolvency, blames cybercrims who shut down production for 6 weeks
blurb: A German company has filed for insolvency after a ransomware attack halted its production lines for six weeks, citing the prolonged outage as the direct cause of its financial collapse. The case is a concrete data point for quantifying downtime risk in business-impact analyses. Extended production stoppage, not the ransom demand itself, drove the firm past recovery, underscoring the need for tested recovery-time objectives on operational-technology systems.

title: Preparing for the post-quantum era: Discover and prioritize now
blurb: HashiCorp argues that post-quantum readiness starts not with swapping algorithms but with discovery: inventorying every cryptographic asset across applications, certificates, machine identities, APIs, and third-party services, then mapping what each protects and its business risk. The urgency is harvest-now-decrypt-later attacks, where adversaries capture RSA- and ECC-protected data today to decrypt once a cryptographically relevant quantum computer exists. Prioritize remediation by the lifetime value of the data each system secures.

title: Pentagon Suspends CMMC Phase 2 as It Rethinks Contractor Cybersecurity Rules
blurb: The Pentagon suspended CMMC 2.0 phase two, which would have required third-party Level 2 certification for new defense contracts starting November 2026, pending a 60-day review citing a shortage of approved assessors. A new task force will collect industry feedback and recommend scaled-back requirements to ease compliance costs for small contractors, though phase one self-assessments and existing FCI and CUI handling rules remain in force.

title: Doc: DHS analysts twice dismissed signs of intruders inside the DHS' network, first detected in May, as harmless activity before confirming a breach in June (David DiMolfetta/Nextgov/FCW)
blurb: DHS security analysts twice flagged and then dismissed signs of intruders on the department's network as benign activity before confirming a breach in June, per an internal document. The initial indicators surfaced in May, meaning attackers dwelled undetected for weeks while triage repeatedly misclassified the malicious traffic, underscoring the cost of alert fatigue and weak escalation criteria in SOC workflows.

title: Cybersecurity M&A Roundup: 37 Deals Announced in June 2026
blurb: SecurityWeek counted 37 cybersecurity M&A deals announced in June 2026, led by Accenture's $4.175B acquisition of a majority stake in Dragos plus full ownership of runZero and NetRise to build an integrated OT security platform. Other notable moves include 1Password buying Apono for $250M to $300M, Databricks acquiring Panther Labs, and Cisco picking up WideField Security to bolster Splunk's identity threat detection.

title: This Android malware can spy on your screen, read your texts, and control your phone remotely
blurb: Group-IB documented an upgraded RedHook Android trojan that abuses Accessibility permissions to enable Wireless ADB and the Shizuku utility, granting itself shell-level privileges and full remote control without root. Delivered via bank- and government-themed smishing that pushes counterfeit Google Play installs, the variant exposes 53 commands for live screen capture, input injection, SMS and contact theft, and silent app management, so defenders should enforce Play Protect and scrutinize Accessibility grants.

title: EU Targets Russian Intelligence Officers Accused of Running a Yearslong Cyber Spying Campaign
blurb: The EU sanctioned nine individuals and four entities tied to Russian military intelligence over a cyber espionage and sabotage campaign running since 2010, centering its measures on the FSB's 16th Centre for controlling multiple cyber threat groups. The activity spans at least nine member states, including infrastructure sabotage against heating and power plants and rail systems in Poland, with France set to summon the Russian ambassador.

title: Valarian Raises $50 Million for Sovereign Infrastructure Control Layer
blurb: Valarian raised $50M in Series A funding led by New Enterprise Associates, adding to $20M in prior seed capital, to expand its ACRA sovereign infrastructure control layer. ACRA sits atop Kubernetes to place each AI model, agent, or workload in its own enclave with workload identity, default-deny segmentation, short-lived secrets, and customer-held encryption keys, running identically across public cloud, on-prem, and air-gapped networks.

title: States are building their own election defense networks as federal support evaporates
blurb: The Trump administration fired two Democratic EAC commissioners on July 10 and issued a 50-state DOJ letter threatening to prosecute officials who retain noncitizens on voter rolls, stripping states of the federal certification and support they relied on for voting-machine security. Several Secretaries of State told CyberScoop their top election threat is now their own federal government, not foreign actors or AI, and are shifting resources to county clerks to run elections without federal backing.

title: Hackers breach Lidl’s IT service provider, steal customer data
blurb: Lidl notified customers in Germany, Belgium, and the Netherlands that attackers breached one of its IT service providers and stole a separately stored file of customer data, including names, phone numbers, email addresses, dates of birth, and customer numbers. Lidl said passwords, billing and delivery addresses, and payment details may also have been exposed, and warned recipients to expect phishing and identity-fraud attempts.

title: Officials once again warn defenders that Russian hackers are targeting network devices
blurb: The US, UK, and 12 other countries issued a joint advisory warning that Russia's FSB Center 16 (Berserk Bear, Static Tundra) is compromising critical infrastructure by exploiting weakly configured routers and old Cisco flaws, including CVE-2018-0171 and CVE-2008-4128. Defenders should disable Cisco Smart Install, enforce stronger authentication, and monitor for anomalous logins using local accounts, as the EU tied the group to Poland's December 2025 grid attack.

title: This one cyber crime group accounted for nearly a fifth of all ransomware attacks in June
blurb: The Gentlemen, a new ransomware-as-a-service operator, overtook Qilin to become the most active group globally in June, accounting for 17% of published attacks amid a 10% overall rise in cyberattacks, per Check Point. NTT researchers note the group uses advanced tooling and proxy infrastructure to accelerate attacks and operate stealthily inside compromised networks, indicating a technical maturity that suggests experienced operators with existing ties across the cybercrime ecosystem.

title: EU and UK blacklist Russia’s cyber operators over efforts to destabilize Europe
blurb: The EU and UK jointly sanctioned dozens of Russian individuals and entities, attributing years of cyber espionage and sabotage against European critical infrastructure to the FSB's 16th Centre, which directs threat groups including Turla. The measures name actors behind a December 2025 OT-focused wiper campaign against Polish energy sites and individuals tied to Lumma Stealer, an infostealer Russia used to source credentials for espionage operations.

title: Microsoft Entra ID authentication getting major shakeup with default passkeys very soon
blurb: Microsoft is making passkeys the default sign-in method for new Entra ID users, shifting cloud-identity authentication away from passwords toward phishing-resistant credentials. Admins should confirm their tenants support passkey registration and update conditional-access and enrollment policies before the change rolls out to align onboarding flows with the new default.

title: Fake smart home residents could stand in for real ones in security research
blurb: Researchers at Leipzig University and ipoque used a language model to role-play a smart home resident, generating timestamped device commands that real home-automation hardware can execute to produce authentic network traffic. The approach yields the encrypted-traffic training data intrusion-detection and traffic-analysis tools need without wiring up real homes. The catch: models produce suspiciously tidy behavior, so detectors trained on synthetic mornings may flag ordinary irregular activity as attacks.

title: The best defense against AI attacks turns out to be a skeptical human
blurb: The 2026 SANS AI Survey of 536 practitioners found generative AI now runs in 78% of security workflows, up from about half a year earlier, yet 63% report significant reliability gaps in threat detection and response. Failures cluster around false positives, missed novel threats, and confident-but-wrong output; two-thirds have been misdirected by AI guidance at least once. The takeaway: verify AI output in proportion to what being wrong costs, and reserve human judgment for novel, context-dependent calls.

title: Mako: A Self-Evolving Agentic Operating System (SE-AOS) for Autonomous Web Exploitation
blurb: Researchers introduced Mako, the first "self-evolving agentic operating system," an autonomous web-exploitation engine from LaunchSafe that observes its own failures, synthesizes new exploit capabilities at runtime, and hot-loads them back into itself. It drove all 104 targets on the public XBOW validation benchmark (26 vulnerability classes) to emit fresh per-build flags, but the authors withheld the payloads, exploit chains, and tool source as dual-use research of concern.

title: NSA revives 'Tailored Access Operations' name for elite hacking unit
blurb: The NSA renamed its Office of Computer Network Operations back to Tailored Access Operations (TAO), the elite offensive unit behind Stuxnet, reversing a 2016 reorganization that had split developers and operators into broader directorates. The change, led by Deputy Director Tim Kosiba, recombines tool development and operations under one roof at Fort Meade to speed up network intrusions against targets in China, Russia, and elsewhere.

title: Wireshark 4.6.7 Released to Patch 12 Vulnerabilities in SSH, TLS, Wi-Fi, and pcapng
blurb: Wireshark 4.6.7 patches 12 vulnerabilities across the SSH, TLS, Wi-Fi (IEEE 802.11), and pcapng dissectors, several of which let a crafted packet or capture file crash the analyzer via a denial-of-service condition. Analysts who run Wireshark against untrusted captures should upgrade, since dissector flaws are triggerable by simply opening or processing a malicious file.

title: Prezta: Provable Remote Execution of Zero-Trust Authorization using SNARKs
blurb: This paper introduces Prezta, an architecture that replaces application gateways in OT environments by evaluating access policies inside a zero-knowledge VM on the client, which emits a SNARK proof that resource-constrained edge devices verify in tens of milliseconds. The prototype runs on RISC Zero, supports XACML 3.0 and JWT claims, and passes 83% of the XACML conformance suite, letting policies evolve without patching edge devices.

title: TFTP Honey Pot Results
blurb: A month-long TFTP honeypot on a $5 VPS logged 20-50 packets daily, nearly all benign scans from seven infosec firms including Shodan and Censys.

title: Effective Synthetic Image Detection via Noise Residual Clustering
blurb: This paper introduces a training-free method for detecting AI-generated images, using Noiseprint++ residual fingerprints fed through a frozen Vision Transformer and clustered with K-Means initialized from just a few real samples. It reaches 82.2% average accuracy across four benchmarks, beating supervised state-of-the-art detectors on generalization to unseen generative models, with the strongest gains on diffusion-based images.

title: UK charges five persons linked to fraud platform behind more than a million scam calls
blurb: The UK's National Crime Agency charged five London residents linked to Russian Coms, a caller ID spoofing platform that generated more than 1.3 million scam calls to 500,000 UK numbers between 2021 and 2024. The service, shut down in March 2024, let fraudsters impersonate banks and law enforcement to steal from an estimated 170,000 victims, with reported losses averaging over £9,400 each.

title: Weak Security Continues to Fuel Russian Cyberattacks
blurb: The UK and EU jointly sanctioned 24 Russian individuals and entities for cyberattacks, election interference, and disinformation, the first such coordinated action between the two, while a joint advisory from US and allied agencies attributed ongoing compromises of weakly protected routers to FSB Center 16. Defense, energy, financial, government, and healthcare organizations are most at risk, and the NSA is urging basic router hygiene to blunt the state-level access.

title: A ransomware negotiator who colluded with attackers to scam victims was sentenced to 70 months in prison
blurb: A federal court sentenced Angelo Martino, a DigitalMint ransomware negotiator, to 70 months in prison for secretly feeding BlackCat (ALPHV) affiliates the confidential negotiating positions of five clients he was hired to represent, inflating ransoms across a scheme that netted over $75M. Prosecutors say Martino and two co-conspirators also deployed ransomware themselves, underscoring insider risk in the third-party IR and negotiation firms defenders routinely trust during active extortion.

title: New Microsoft Defender update can let hackers totally fill your Windows 11 PC disk space
blurb: A recent Microsoft Defender update introduces a flaw that lets an attacker completely exhaust a Windows 11 machine's disk space, degrading the endpoint into a denial-of-service state. No CVE has been assigned yet and Microsoft has not shipped a fix, so admins should monitor Defender-related disk consumption and hold the update in managed environments until a corrected build is available.

title: Cloudflare now studies your mouse clicks in CAPTCHA bot gambit
blurb: Cloudflare released Precursor, a session-based bot management tool that uses client-side JavaScript to continuously analyze behavioral signals like mouse paths, corrections, overshoots, and timing variations to distinguish humans from bots in real time. Session scoping prevents bots from resetting their behavioral signature by refreshing the page or requesting a new CAPTCHA, and Cloudflare says the signals aren't exposed to dashboards or linked to user identities.

title: FFmpeg fixes PixelSmash flaw in widely used video decoder
blurb: FFmpeg patched PixelSmash (CVE-2026-8461), a high-severity heap out-of-bounds write in the MagicYUV decoder that JFrog used to achieve remote code execution on a Jellyfin media server through its normal library-scan pipeline. Any application built on libavcodec is exposed, including Kodi, OBS Studio, Nextcloud, PhotoPrism, and Linux desktop thumbnail generators, so IT teams should prioritize updating FFmpeg across self-hosted media and file-preview infrastructure.

title: Securing the AI supply chain on GKE: Introducing k8s-aibom for automated AI BOMs
blurb: Google open-sourced k8s-aibom, a lightweight unprivileged Kubernetes controller for GKE that continuously scans cluster workloads to detect running AI runtimes like vLLM and Triton and auto-generate CycloneDX 1.6 ML-BOMs. The tool ships with no sidecars, eBPF modules, or privileged DaemonSets, giving security teams audit-grade visibility into shadow AI without adding developer integration friction or requiring pod-spec changes.

title: The US government warns that Russia state hackers are coming after your router
blurb: CISA and allied cyber agencies from the UK, Australia, Denmark, and New Zealand warned that Russia's FSB Center 16 group is mass-compromising poorly configured routers, exploiting SNMP agents with default or common credentials to enroll edge devices into proxy botnets. The advisory names critical-infrastructure operators as targets, putting pressure on IT teams to audit SNMP exposure and rotate default credentials on home, small-office, and network-edge hardware.

title: When checking the URL isn't enough: a Device Code Phishing attack via a Microsoft website
blurb: Attackers are running phishing campaigns that exploit Microsoft's legitimate Device Authorization Grant flow, tricking victims into entering an attacker-generated device code at the real login.microsoftonline.com and handing over live session tokens. Because the victim authenticates on a trusted Microsoft domain, standard "check the URL" guidance and many detection tools miss the attack, leaving Entra ID tenants exposed unless admins restrict the device code flow via Conditional Access.

title: Lessons Learned from CISA’s Recent GitHub Leak
blurb: CISA published a postmortem on a leak in which a contractor left dozens of internal credentials, including AWS GovCloud administrative keys and plaintext passwords, in a public GitHub repository for nearly six months before a researcher flagged it. The agency took over 48 hours to rotate the exposed keys and now recommends teams maintain well-tested key management and publish clear, redundant reporting channels for outside researchers.

title: Introducing Precursor: detecting agentic behavior with continuous client-side signals
blurb: Cloudflare launched Precursor, a client-side session-based verification system that injects JavaScript to continuously collect behavioral signals and separate human traffic from bots and AI agents across the full user journey. It ships as an optional complement to Turnstile within Enterprise Bot Management, extending detection beyond login and checkout endpoints to the entire application without adding friction for legitimate users.

title: Microsoft Maps Three Salesforce Attack Paths Tied to a Year of ShinyHunters Activity
blurb: Microsoft published research on July 13 mapping a year of Salesforce intrusions, tied to methods matching the extortion group ShinyHunters, to three techniques: vishing employees into approving malicious OAuth apps, stolen tokens from compromised vendors, and misconfigured guest access. None exploited a platform flaw, so Microsoft and Salesforce rolled out new detection and governance tooling to catch abuse that authentication logs miss.

title: Hackers backdoor Jscrambler npm package with infostealer malware
blurb: Jscrambler disclosed that attackers used compromised npm publishing credentials to push a backdoored version of its Code Integrity package, which ran an infostealer during the preinstall hook and was downloaded 1,479 times in a two-hour window before deprecation. The malware harvested developer credentials, cloud secrets, CI/CD tokens, MCP and AI-tool configurations, and crypto wallets, extending supply-chain risk to any enterprise dev team that pulled the affected 8.14 through 8.20 releases.

title: US and allies warn of Russian critical infrastructure attacks
blurb: Nineteen cybersecurity agencies from the US, UK, and seven other countries jointly attributed a campaign against critical infrastructure routers to Russia's FSB Center 16, which scans for devices with default or weak SNMP strings and exploits the 2018 Cisco Smart Install flaw CVE-2018-0171. IT teams in energy, communications, healthcare, and government are urged to move to SNMPv3, disable Smart Install, block TFTP at the edge, and replace end-of-life devices.

title: New York becomes the first state to enact a data center moratorium
blurb: New York became the first state to enact a data center moratorium, with Governor Kathy Hochul signing an executive action that blocks new environmental permits for facilities over 50 megawatts for up to a year. The pause gives the Department of Public Service time to draft standards for energy and water impact, and puts hyperscale expansion by AWS, Microsoft, and Google on hold in a major market while a stricter 20-megawatt legislative bill awaits her signature.

title: New CrashStealer malware poses as Apple crash reporting tool
blurb: New macOS infostealer CrashStealer impersonates Apple's CrashReporter tool and ships inside a signed, Apple-notarized installer that clears Gatekeeper without warnings, then harvests Keychain secrets, browser credentials, 80-plus crypto wallets, and 14 password managers including 1Password and Bitwarden. Jamf tracked the malware since May and observed live attacks in early July, underscoring that notarization is no longer a reliable trust signal for endpoint and MDM defenses.

title: Japan's largest taxi operator shuts systems after cyberattack
blurb: Nihon Kotsu, Japan's largest taxi and chauffeur operator with roughly $1B in annual revenue, shut down parts of its infrastructure after detecting a malware infection over the weekend, taking its taxi dispatch, web booking, and telephone reservation systems offline. The company has engaged external cybersecurity experts and is investigating possible data theft, with no ransomware group yet claiming responsibility.

title: EU and UK officially blame Russian spies for cyberattack on Poland's power grid
blurb: The EU and UK formally attributed the cyberattack on Poland's power grid to Russian state-backed hackers, marking the first coordinated bloc-level attribution over an energy-sector intrusion. The move sets up potential sanctions and raises compliance pressure on European utilities and their critical-infrastructure suppliers to meet NIS2 resilience requirements.

title: Progress orders emergency ShareFile server shutdown over mystery security threat
blurb: Progress Software ordered customers to immediately shut down on-prem ShareFile servers over an undisclosed security threat, without publishing details of the flaw or a patch. The blind directive lands as Progress is still repairing its reputation from the 2023 MOVEit mass-exploitation, and enterprises running ShareFile for regulated file transfer now face unplanned downtime with no timeline for a fix.

title: Europe strikes out against Russia’s Turla over espionage, ‘destructive attacks’
blurb: The EU, its member states, and the UK sanctioned Russian individuals and entities over a Turla cyberespionage campaign running since 2010 and FSB-linked attacks that knocked out heat for 500,000 people on Poland's energy grid last December. The UK's first sanctions coordinated with the EU named GRU leadership and Lumma Stealer operators, signaling that critical-infrastructure operators across the bloc remain live targets for state-directed intrusions.

title: NCSC issues warning over Russian intelligence-backed threat group
blurb: The NCSC, alongside 18 partner agencies, issued a joint advisory warning that Russia's FSB Centre 16 group (also tracked as Berserk Bear and Static Tundra) is opportunistically compromising vulnerable routers and critical infrastructure via SNMP scans and Cisco Smart Install flaws. Organizations in communications, energy, healthcare, defense, and financial services should patch exposed network devices and disable legacy protocols immediately, as the UK simultaneously sanctions 24 entities tied to the campaigns.

title: Deluxe Corporation beats the odds with mainframe migration using AI
blurb: Deluxe Corporation migrated off a 50-year-old mainframe to the cloud in about 12 months, using AI to rewrite legacy code, generate documentation, and build test cases for a projected $4.9M in annual savings. The result runs against Gartner's forecast that over 70% of 2026 mainframe-exit projects will fail from overreliance on gen AI, with Deluxe treating the tooling as one component rather than the whole migration.

title: New York Set to Temporarily Ban Large New Data Centers
blurb: New York is set to temporarily ban construction of large new data centers for up to a year, as Gov. Kathy Hochul pauses permits to write environmental and energy-grid regulations. The moratorium adds site-selection risk for hyperscalers and enterprises planning New York capacity, pushing AI and cloud buildout toward states with faster approvals and firmer power commitments.

title: Microsoft Entra ID authentication overhaul to start in September 2026
blurb: Microsoft will make passkeys the default authentication method for Entra ID in the public cloud on September 1, 2026, and starting February 1, 2027, users on SMS or voice MFA must register a passkey to sign in, with no tenant opt-out. Microsoft will also retire its native SMS and voice delivery, pushing organizations that still need it to contract and pay for third-party telecom providers through the Microsoft Security Store.

title: Google Credential Provider for Windows (GCPW) now supports FIDO2-compliant physical security keys as a second factor for authentication
blurb: Google Workspace added FIDO2-compliant hardware security key support to Google Credential Provider for Windows, letting admins enforce 2-Step Verification with physical keys at the Windows login screen. The update, available to all Workspace customers with no end-user setup, also accepts passkeys from nearby Bluetooth-connected mobile devices and gives Windows-heavy shops phishing-resistant MFA without a third-party identity provider.

title: Governments to enterprises: Improve your router security hygiene
blurb: Nineteen government cybersecurity agencies across North America, the UK, Europe, and Australia issued a joint advisory warning that Russian state-sponsored actors are compromising critical infrastructure by exploiting weak SNMP configurations, default credentials, and unpatched Cisco flaws in enterprise routers. The advisory flags decade-old vectors like CVE-2018-0171 and Smart Install abuse, pressing IT teams to rotate community strings, retire SNMPv1/v2, and patch neglected network devices.

title: Cloudflare launches Precursor to catch bots by watching entire sessions
blurb: Cloudflare made Precursor generally available, a bot-detection system that scores visitor behavior across an entire browsing session rather than running a single point-of-entry CAPTCHA. Enterprise customers already routing traffic through Cloudflare enable it with one click and no code changes, and its continuous Bot Score closes the page-reload evasion that resets per-request challenges, extending Cloudflare's bot-management stack against automation it pegs at 57% of web requests.

title: Microsoft is losing the battle to protect license lucre. It better get used to the feeling
blurb: The author argues Microsoft can no longer defend the high-margin licensing revenue that has anchored its business, as customers shift toward AI tooling, open ecosystems, and consumption-based models it doesn't control. For enterprise buyers, the takeaway is leverage: the pricing power that made Windows and Office seat licenses non-negotiable is eroding, and the piece contends Microsoft will have to accept thinner license lock-in as the new baseline.

title: Walmart bets on AI and digital twins to shape its supply chain strategy
blurb: Walmart is deploying AI and digital twin simulations across its supply chain to model logistics scenarios and route products around weather disruptions and geopolitical conflict. The retailer's approach, described by supply chain tech lead Indira Uppuluri, treats predictive modeling as core infrastructure for maintaining product availability across its global store and distribution network.

title: India’s tech services giant HCL is getting into the AI datacenter business
blurb: HCL Technologies is entering the AI datacenter business, expanding beyond its core IT services and outsourcing operations into infrastructure buildout. The move puts India's third-largest tech services firm into direct competition with hyperscalers and specialized AI-cloud providers, betting that its enterprise client base will source AI compute from the same vendor already running their applications.

title: Microsoft chief turns hostile on frontier AI labs, warns companies to guard their IP
blurb: Satya Nadella publicly warned enterprises to lock down their intellectual property when working with frontier AI labs like OpenAI and Anthropic, cautioning that proprietary data and IP can leak into model training. The stance is notable given Microsoft's multibillion-dollar OpenAI stake, and it signals to CIOs that vendor AI partnerships now carry data-governance and IP-protection clauses worth scrutinizing before signing.

title: Microsoft emails Windows 10 holdouts: Fine, keep your old PC another year
blurb: Microsoft emailed Windows 10 holdouts offering a path to keep receiving security updates for another year past the October 2025 end-of-support cutoff, softening its push to force upgrades onto Windows 11-capable hardware. For IT leaders managing fleets with machines that fail Windows 11's TPM and CPU requirements, the extension buys planning time but adds another Extended Security Updates line item to budget rather than removing the migration deadline.

title: Pure DC Building Finland AI Data Center, Secures Microsoft Deal
blurb: Pure Data Centres, backed by Oaktree Capital Management, is building an AI data center in Finland that Microsoft and other clients are slated to use. The project is part of a wider push to expand European digital infrastructure for AI, giving Microsoft added regional capacity as it scales Azure and Copilot workloads inside EU data-residency boundaries.

title: CIOs must rethink operating models to unlock AI at scale
blurb: The AI arrow is racing ahead of the organizations, people, and data meant to support it, and this analysis argues the readiness gap is operational, not technical. It cites Lopez Research finding 83% of firms name data quality as their top AI challenge and 74% struggle to show ROI, while only 21% report a mature agent governance model per Deloitte. The takeaway for CIOs: fix data foundations, operating models, and governance yourself rather than waiting for vendors to close the gap.

title: States are building their own election defense networks as federal support evaporates
blurb: The Trump administration fired two Election Assistance Commission commissioners and sent a DOJ letter threatening states with criminal prosecution over voter-roll management, prompting state election officials to build independent election-security networks as federal support withdraws. Secretaries of state told CyberScoop the top threat to their elections is now the federal government itself, as the EAC's voluntary voting-machine certification, which states rely on for procurement, loses its bipartisan standing.

title: Data centers gobbled up 23% of Ireland’s electricity supply in 2025 – nearly as much power as every home in the country combined
blurb: Irish data centers consumed 23% of Ireland's electricity in 2025, up from 18% the prior year, hitting 7,663 GWh and nearly matching the combined usage of every home in the country, according to Central Statistics Office figures. The concentration, driven by 80-plus facilities clustered around Dublin, raises grid-capacity and siting risks that enterprises planning Irish or EU data center footprints now have to price into expansion decisions.

title: Microsoft Entra ID authentication getting major shakeup with default passkeys very soon
blurb: Microsoft is making passkeys the default sign-in method for Entra ID, shifting new users to phishing-resistant authentication without a separate rollout decision from admins. IT teams standardized on Entra get stronger default security across their workforce, but should prepare for enrollment support and fallback policies before the change takes effect.

title: Huawei eyes DRAM production to combat memory shortage: report
blurb: Huawei is reportedly building a 12-inch wafer fab in Shenzhen with local memory maker Swaysure, targeting 140,000 wafers per month on a 28nm process focused primarily on DRAM. The project, said to have Beijing's backing, is a bid to ease China's memory shortage under U.S. export limits that bar advanced DRAM and NAND, and advances China's goal of semiconductor self-sufficiency by 2030.

title: How the memory shortage hits CIOs as Apple boosts hardware prices
blurb: The global memory and storage chip shortage, driven by AI infrastructure demand, is pushing up enterprise PC and server costs, with Apple raising Mac prices 15% to 20% and iPad prices as much as 25% while Lenovo, Dell, and HP already hiked device prices earlier. CIOs are responding by stretching refresh cycles, with ETS moving laptops from a four-year to a six-year cadence and using OS reinstalls to extend device lifespans.

title: Copilot could soon help diagnose issues with your PC
blurb: Microsoft is testing PC Insights, an opt-in Copilot feature that answers natural-language questions about a PC's hardware by reading CPU, RAM, GPU, and storage usage, connected devices, and Bluetooth and Wi-Fi state. The tool only flags problems rather than fixing them, and its pitch is undercut by the Copilot app itself, which Windows Latest measured using up to 1GB of RAM at idle.

title: Your Azure DevOps pipelines have a 2027 deadline, and the clock has just started
blurb: Microsoft deprecated the Azure DevOps issuer for workload identity federation on July 1, 2026, with full retirement set for July 1, 2027, and every Azure Pipelines Service Connection built before late 2025 needs migrating to the Entra issuer or it stops authenticating to Azure. The portal converts connections one at a time, so shops with hundreds of single-tenant connections in the public cloud will need to script the undocumented MigrateToEntraIssuer REST call to finish before the deadline.

title: BitTitan expands MigrationWiz with Microsoft Teams private chat migrations
blurb: BitTitan added Microsoft Teams private chat migration to its MigrationWiz platform, letting IT teams move one-on-one and group chat histories alongside mailboxes, files, and channel data during Microsoft 365 tenant-to-tenant moves. The addition closes a common gap in merger, acquisition, and divestiture migrations, where preserving Teams chat continuity previously required manual workarounds or third-party tools outside the MigrationWiz workflow.

title: Weather grows as one of data center growth’s greatest risks
blurb: Zurich North America's new report names severe weather as the top construction risk for AI data centers, citing that 64% of US capacity under construction now sits outside traditional hubs like Northern Virginia, in tornado- and hail-prone regions such as West Texas, Tennessee, and Ohio. Weather now drives 32% of losses in Zurich's data center portfolio, surpassing fire, as insured campus values jump from $150M five years ago to roughly $3B today.

title: Up the Stack: How AI's Escape From the Commodity Trap Risks Enterprise Lock-in
blurb: The authors argue the AI bubble debate misses the point: inference is a commodity business with low switching costs and undifferentiated models, so the leading labs are migrating up the stack to escape it. That move lets them build defensible margins but reintroduces the enterprise concerns buyers thought AI had priced away. For IT leaders, the takeaway is that today's cheap, swappable model APIs are a transitional phase, not the long-run cost structure.

title: Securing the AI supply chain on GKE: Introducing k8s-aibom for automated AI BOMs
blurb: Google open-sourced k8s-aibom, an unprivileged Kubernetes controller that watches the cluster API to detect running AI runtimes like vLLM and Triton and generate CycloneDX 1.6 ML-BOMs without sidecars, eBPF, or pod-spec edits. It routes the resulting bill of materials to an in-cluster custom resource and optional sinks like Cloud Storage, giving security teams audit-grade visibility into shadow AI workloads that skip formal registration.

title: Modernizing the Meta Ads Service With an Open-Source Kernel Scheduler
blurb: Meta's Ads and Linux Kernel teams replaced the general-purpose EEVDF scheduler with a workload-aware policy built on sched_ext, the BPF-based extensible scheduling framework that landed in kernel v6.12. By encoding thread priority directly into the scheduler so latency-critical work runs first, they cut ads-retrieval p99 latency 28%, saved 3.28MW of power, and ranked 1.1% more ads. The move also cleared technical debt from ad hosts stuck on the older v6.4 kernel to dodge an EEVDF latency regression.

title: Operating OpenTelemetry at scale with OpAMP
blurb: OpAMP, the Open Agent Management Protocol, gives observability teams a standardized way to remotely configure, update, and health-check fleets of OpenTelemetry Collectors from a central backend. In this OpenObservability Talks episode, BindPlane's Andy Keller explains why in-house management protocols kept fragmenting across HTTP polling, WebSockets, and protobufs, and how OpAMP replaces them for deployments ranging from a few gateways to millions of collectors on embedded and point-of-sale devices.

title: Introducing GuardDog 3.0: A new rules engine, transparent sandboxing, and more
blurb: Datadog released GuardDog 3.0, an open-source scanner for malicious PyPI, npm, and Go packages that swaps Semgrep for YARA via yara-python, cutting memory use and per-file process spawning on large packages. The release adds a risk-scoring engine that correlates heuristics into a single maliciousness confidence rather than dumping one unranked finding per rule match.

title: Code review is theater now
blurb: The author argues that code review has become theater in the era of AI-generated code, since agents produce PRs faster than any human can meaningfully evaluate whether the code should exist, not just whether it compiles. Reviewers end up checking syntax and structure while missing patterns the model copied from training data that don't fit the problem, and atomizing a 40,000-line change into hundreds of small diffs doesn't fix the underlying scale mismatch.

title: The four horsemen behind thousands of Postgres outages
blurb: The author argues that most Postgres outages trace back to a handful of predictable failure modes, starting with vacuum and transaction ID wraparound. Because Postgres marks deleted rows rather than removing them, the background vacuum must reclaim space and old transaction IDs, competing with query I/O; fall too far behind and Postgres shuts the database down to avoid corruption. He is prototyping fixes in pgrust, a Postgres reimplementation.

title: RabbitMQ Vulnerability Threatens Enterprise Systems
blurb: Miggo disclosed CVE-2026-57219 (CVSS 8.7) in RabbitMQ, an obsolete management endpoint that hands the broker's OAuth client secret to any unauthenticated caller who can reach the management port, letting an attacker mint an admin token and take over users, queues, and settings. Deployments using an OIDC provider like Keycloak or Entra ID should patch to 4.2.6 or 3.13.15 and rotate the secret immediately.

title: Show HN: Clawk – Give coding agents a disposable Linux VM, not your laptop
blurb: Clawk gives coding agents their own disposable Linux VM instead of your laptop, mounting your repo with root in the guest and no permission prompts while your keychain and host files stay unreachable. A network allow-list blocks connections to unknown servers, and clawk destroy && clawk rebuilds a fresh VM against the same repo with --resume restoring the conversation after an agent wrecks the box.

title: Operating AI/ML Workloads on Kubernetes: A Headlamp Plugin for Kubeflow
blurb: The Kubernetes SIG UI team released a Headlamp plugin for Kubeflow that surfaces Kubeflow CRDs like TrainJob, Katib Experiments, and pipeline Runs directly inside Headlamp's general-purpose Kubernetes UI. It reads straight from the API server to show Pod conditions and failure reasons such as ImagePullBackOff or OOMKilled, sparing operators the drop back to kubectl when an ML notebook or training run stalls.

title: Kubernetes Dashboard to Headlamp: A Step-by-Step Guide
blurb: The Kubernetes blog published a step-by-step guide for migrating from Kubernetes Dashboard to Headlamp, framing the shift as moving from a cluster-bound web app to a kubeconfig-driven client that follows your identity. The operational trade-offs: login moves from pasted service-account tokens to kubeconfig and SSO, resource creation shifts from forms to apply-YAML, and multi-cluster views become the default rather than one Dashboard per cluster. The guide includes a pre-migration checklist to prove RBAC and access parity before decommissioning Dashboard.

title: TerraRepair: A Tool-Grounded LLM Agent for Infrastructure-as-Code Repair
blurb: TerraRepair is a tool-grounded LLM agent that repairs Terraform misconfigurations flagged by scanners like Checkov and Trivy, retrieving dependency context and provider schemas and re-running the scanner before returning a fix. On combined AWS benchmarks it lifted scanner-verified fix rates from 26.6% to 78.4% on Checkov and 44.8% to 72.4% on Trivy versus a one-shot baseline. When deployment-specific context is missing, the agent escalates to a human instead of fabricating a plausible fix or silently suppressing the warning.

title: Cloudflare launches Precursor to catch bots by watching entire sessions
blurb: Cloudflare made Precursor generally available, a bot-detection system that scores visitor behavior across an entire session rather than running a single point-in-time CAPTCHA at arrival. A one-click script logs mouse movement, scroll rhythm, and typing cadence as timing patterns, then flags contradictions like pointer activity on a hidden tab, and a running Bot Score follows the visitor so a page reload no longer wipes the automated signature.

title: ClickHouse on Docker Hardened Images
blurb: ClickHouse is now available as Docker Hardened Images, security-stripped builds of the server, ClickHouse Keeper, the Kubernetes Operator and Helm chart, and the metrics exporter that ship only what each component needs to run. The hardened images drop the Ubuntu base packages like wget and Perl that trigger unfixable CVEs in Trivy, Grype, and ECR scans, clearing the security gate without changing how the database runs.

title: Apple acquires observability startup SigScalr
blurb: Apple acquired certain assets of SigScalr and hired its team, the startup behind SigLens, an open-source observability platform pitched as a cheaper alternative to Splunk, Datadog, and Elasticsearch for logs, metrics, and traces. SigScalr's site is offline and the SigLens repo has been archived read-only under Apache 2.0, leaving teams that adopted it to fork or migrate off.

title: Your Azure DevOps pipelines have a 2027 deadline, and the clock has just started
blurb: Microsoft deprecated the Azure DevOps issuer for workload identity federation on July 1, 2026, giving teams until July 1, 2027 before Service Connections built on the old vstoken.dev.azure.com issuer stop authenticating to Azure. The portal migrates connections one at a time in a couple of minutes each, so orgs running hundreds will need the undocumented MigrateToEntraIssuer REST call, and only single-tenant Entra apps and Managed Identities in the public cloud are affected.

title: Introducing Smart Retry: Safer retries for transient Jenkins failures.
blurb: Jenkins released Smart Retry, a Pipeline step that classifies a failed build into deterministic categories like AGENT_LOST, SCM_TRANSIENT, or COMPILATION_FAILURE before deciding whether to rerun it. Configurable profiles keep retries conservative by default, recovering from transient infrastructure faults while failing fast on real code errors and user aborts, so teams cut wasted reruns without masking genuine breakage.

title: How to protect your MCP Server with Fastly
blurb: Fastly documented an architecture for putting MCP servers behind its edge network, built after a global publisher asked it to secure the MCP backbone connecting AI agents to real-time financial data. The prototype enforces MCP-specific controls that conventional API protection misses, blocking unexpected content types like XML and restricting HTTP methods at ingress, while holding sub-100ms latency and 100Mbps throughput across mixed IP, token, and mTLS auth. It reads as a reference for teams treating MCP as a new, dynamic attack surface rather than deterministic API traffic.

title: Instagram will charge for AI access
blurb: Instagram head Adam Mosseri confirmed that Meta will move its in-app generative AI tools, including Muse-powered image and video effects, to a paid subscription model once users exceed daily free caps. The shift signals that AI-native creative features marketers rely on for organic Instagram content are becoming a gated cost center, not a free platform utility.

title: EU takes next steps to limit teen social media use
blurb: The European Commission advanced new measures to restrict minors' access to social media, including proposed age-verification requirements and a push for a harmonized EU-wide digital minimum age. The move signals tighter targeting and data-collection limits for platforms like Meta, TikTok, and Snap, meaning marketers reaching Gen Z audiences in Europe should expect narrower addressable segments and more friction in youth-focused campaigns.

title: Exclusive: Peec rival Promptwatch raises €6m seed round
blurb: Amsterdam-based Promptwatch raised a €6m seed round led by Seed + Speed Ventures to expand its generative engine optimization tooling into New York, one year after hitting €2m ARR with nearly 2,000 users including Duolingo and Fireflies. GEO has become a distinct marketing priority as chatbots like ChatGPT and Claude cite brands with none of the keyword-level transparency marketers relied on in traditional search.

title: Is Open Semantic Interchange your data silo cure?
blurb: Open Semantic Interchange is an emerging standard that aims to make an "account," "lead," or "campaign metric" mean the same thing across every platform in a martech stack, replacing the custom middleware enterprises build to reconcile Salesforce, HubSpot, and Marketo field definitions. Adopting it is not plug-and-play, requiring ops teams to first audit every custom data object and then map internal taxonomies to shared OSI schemas before silos actually break down.

title: Why CIPA is changing the conversation around digital tracking
blurb: A 1967 California wiretapping law, CIPA, is resurfacing in lawsuits over website tracking pixels and session-replay tools, exposing companies to civil penalties of up to $5,000 per violation per day and a private right of action for any California resident. Legal teams are now pulling marketers into compliance conversations to map which analytics and ad-tech tools collect data, since exposure extends to any company communicating with California users.

title: Google built the SEO industry, AI is dismantling it, and Promptwatch just landed €6M to help brands survive the shift
blurb: Promptwatch raised €6M in seed funding led by seed + speed Ventures to help brands track and optimize how AI models describe them, as Gartner projects organic search traffic could fall 50% or more by 2028. The Amsterdam startup hit €2M ARR within a year by processing over 100 million daily prompt-response data points, targeting the metrics vacuum left as keywords, rankings, and CTR lose meaning in AI search.

title: Scannable product tags vs. none
blurb: Klaviyo A/B tested scannable product tags against standard product cards on its homepage, and the tags won.

title: What Al Pacino can teach you about copy
blurb: A copywriting breakdown of Al Pacino's "game of inches" speech from Any Given Sunday argues that the coach's persuasion techniques map directly onto conversion copy: opening quiet instead of shouting, and making admissions against self-interest to earn belief. The Volkswagen "Think Small" parallel reinforces the tactic of pulling readers in by underselling rather than crowding the page. The through-line for CRO teams is that small, believable moves compound, since a slight edge is all a conversion rate needs.

title: How Decagon Uses AI for Design System Saturation
blurb: Decagon documented how its design team scaled a Figma library to hundreds of components by treating design files as precise inputs for coding agents, using Figma MCP and Figma Make to feed agents consistent context. The payoff was operational: with an org-wide source of truth, designers and engineers stopped debating which button or table to use and shared a vocabulary, and the library logged tens of thousands of component inserts in 30 days. The takeaway for PMs is that agentic coding raises the stakes on design-system hygiene, since inconsistent inputs now propagate directly into shipped code.

title: ECB Digital Euro Trial Enlists Firms from Revolut to UniCredit
blurb: The ECB enlisted 36 payment service providers, including Revolut, UniCredit, and Deutsche Bank, to run a digital euro trial testing conditional payments and offline settlement. The pilot moves the central bank digital currency toward a possible 2027 rollout, positioning European banks as distribution rails rather than issuers as the ECB retains control over the currency itself.

title: Hyperliquid’s HIP-3 markets surge to nearly 50% of perp volume as onchain stock trading grows
blurb: Hyperliquid's HIP-3 permissionless markets now account for nearly 50% of the protocol's total perpetuals volume, up from roughly 2% at the start of the year, as onchain equity trading gains traction. The jump reflects growing demand for tokenized stock perps and other builder-deployed markets, shifting a larger share of Hyperliquid's flow away from its core crypto pairs.

title: Bonzo Lend Loses $9M on Hedera in Supra Oracle Exploit
blurb: Bonzo Lend, a lending protocol on Hedera, lost about $9.05M on July 11 after an attacker exploited a verification flaw in a third-party Supra oracle, using a few dollars of SAUCE collateral and a manipulated price feed to borrow 6.63M USDC and 34.52M wrapped HBAR. Hedera's TVL fell nearly 40% in 24 hours while Bonzo's dropped 77% to $3.06M, with over $5.25M of the stolen funds bridged to Ethereum and swapped into ETH.

title: Compromised Injective SDK npm Package Exfiltrates Wallet Keys and Mnemonics
blurb: Socket detected a malicious `@injectivelabs/sdk-ts@1.20.21` release on npm that exfiltrates wallet private keys and mnemonics through fake telemetry code, introduced via commits from a compromised maintainer account with an established contribution history. The write-up traces the full timeline, including the attacker's `test-backdoor-check` branch and the real owner's fast containment, and notes the blast radius extended to 17 additional `@injectivelabs` scoped packages that pinned the malicious version, exposing transitive users who never installed the SDK directly.

title: Sony secures conditional approval to set up US stablecoin trust bank
blurb: Sony Bank received preliminary conditional OCC approval to form Connectia Trust, a New York subsidiary capitalized with $40 million to issue and manage dollar-denominated stablecoins. It joins Bridge, Paxos, and Circle in winning federal trust-bank clearance under the GENIUS Act framework, though operations stay on hold pending final OCC sign-off in a market where USDT and USDC already hold roughly $250 billion.

title: President Trump, White House press Senate to advance the Clarity Act while ethics fight looms
blurb: Trump and White House officials pressed the Senate to advance the Clarity Act, which would establish the first federal framework for crypto market structure, with new bill text expected this week ahead of a possible full-chamber vote. The House passed its version last year and key Senate committees have cleared theirs, but an ethics fight and a compressed legislative calendar still stand between the bill and final passage.

title: SBI Holdings, Solana Foundation partner to build Japan-based onchain financial market
blurb: SBI Holdings and the Solana Foundation partnered to build a Japan-based onchain financial market, with SBI's R3 joint venture rebranding to SBI Solana Global. The tie-up gives Solana a regulated distribution channel into Japanese institutional finance through one of the country's largest financial groups.

title: BlackRock, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley join UK government's tokenization taskforce
blurb: The UK Treasury launched a tokenization taskforce of 54 financial firms, including BlackRock, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley, HSBC, and UBS, that will spend the next year building live use cases across UK markets, starting with tokenized repo. The initiative, backed by the City of London Corporation and led by former FCA chair Chris Woolard, targets an RWA market that BCG estimates could reach $88 trillion by 2035.

title: Cardano's Voltaire Governance: Complete Specification and Research Program
blurb: This technical report specifies Cardano's Voltaire on-chain governance system, introduced via CIP-1694 and activated by the September 2024 Chang hard fork, covering its three-body architecture, seven governance action types, voting rules, and constitutional framework in enough detail for implementation or formal analysis. The authors also lay out a research agenda, including a minimal executable governance kernel modeling self-amendment as a state-transition system for safety and liveness analysis. Voltaire currently manages a treasury of roughly $235M (1.47B ADA).

title: Time Is Money: Incentivized Causal Transaction Ordering
blurb: This paper introduces PRECEDE, a mechanism-design approach to blockchain front-running that enforces causal transaction ordering by removing the economic incentive to reorder. Instead of ordering strictly by bid, PRECEDE runs a power-weighted randomized lottery whose winning probability grows super-linearly in the bid, creating an equilibrium where users publish a deterring bid and attackers refrain from competing. The authors show it also neutralizes sandwich attacks and deploys on any censorship-resistant chain with a single change to the ordering rule.

title: US banking groups urge Senate to strengthen stablecoin provisions in Clarity Act
blurb: US banking industry groups urged the Senate to tighten the stablecoin provisions in the Clarity Act, warning that the bill as written could let stablecoins substitute for bank deposits and trigger deposit flight from community banks. The push adds a new front to the market-structure fight, pitting incumbent lenders against issuers over whether payment stablecoins compete directly with insured deposits.

title: Bolivia weighs adding Tether's USDT to its national payments system
blurb: Bolivia is evaluating a framework to integrate Tether's USDT into its national payments system as a regulated alternative circulating alongside the boliviano and the US dollar. The move follows a 630% jump in crypto transaction volume after the central bank lifted restrictions in June 2024, though official adoption hinges on stronger anti-money-laundering controls given Bolivia's spot on the FATF grey list.

title: Robinhood Chain draws over $3 billion in weekly DEX volume to join top five chains: Bernstein
blurb: Robinhood Chain drew roughly $3.1 billion in cumulative DEX volume during its first seven days live, ranking among the top five chains by decentralized exchange activity, according to a Bernstein research note. Since the July 1 mainnet launch, more than 65,000 users hold $300 million in stablecoins and $13 million in tokenized stocks on the Arbitrum-based Ethereum L2, with meme coins driving early speculative flow.

title: Japan’s largest security token platform moves nearly $3 billion to Avalanche blockchain
blurb: Progmat, Japan's dominant security token platform spun out of MUFG, completed its migration of over ¥452 billion (about $2.7 billion) in tokenized assets from a permissioned Corda 5 ledger to a dedicated public Avalanche Layer 1. The move makes rights-transfer processing 3 to 5 times faster with sub-two-second finality, and opens Progmat's tokenized real estate and corporate bonds, roughly 64.6% of Japan's issuance value, to EVM interoperability.

title: UK lays out tokenized finance roadmap with projected £33 billion annual boost
blurb: The UK Treasury published a roadmap for tokenized finance, projecting a £33 billion annual boost to the economy through wholesale settlement and asset digitization. The framework calls for payment infrastructure to modernize alongside tokenized assets, with industry participants pointing to real-time settlement as the prerequisite for the projected gains.

title: UK Treasury report on tokenization cites Ripple as convergence model
blurb: A UK Treasury-backed report on wholesale digital markets named Ripple among the credentialed firms in a 12-month plan to move tokenized repo, gilts and funds from sandbox pilots into live markets. The report proposes a hybrid model layering permissioned institutional networks atop permissionless chains, citing Ripple's $1.25B Hidden Road acquisition and Santander UK's use of its rails as evidence of TradFi-crypto convergence.

title: 7 ways to drain an ERC-4626 vault (and how a good protocol closes each one)
blurb: A dev.to post walks through seven ways to drain an ERC-4626 tokenized vault, drawn from audits of production vaults and AMMs, and how a correct implementation closes each. The list covers first-depositor share inflation, rounding that favors the withdrawer, manipulable totalAssets via donations or oracles, reentrancy in delegatecall hooks, fee-accounting ordering bugs, weak upgrade access control, and unsocialized bad debt. The author's throughline is that vault safety comes from discipline in rounding, access control, and accounting rather than any single design idea.

title: SBI Holdings' blockchain initiative pivots to Solana for tokenization, stablecoin issuance
blurb: SBI Holdings' blockchain venture, rebranded from SBI R3 Japan to SBI Solana Global, shifted from R3's permissioned Corda chain to Solana for stablecoin issuance and RWA tokenization, adding the Solana Foundation as a joint-venture partner alongside SMFG. The pivot targets connecting Japan's domestic market to global onchain liquidity, and follows SBI's roughly $289M agreement to acquire crypto exchange Bitbank last month.

title: Strategy pauses its Bitcoin buying spree to hoard a massive $3 billion cash cushion
blurb: Strategy has not bought bitcoin since June 22 and instead sold 3,588 BTC for roughly $216 million, lifting its US dollar reserve to $3 billion and cutting its holdings to 843,775 BTC. The cushion covers about 20.4 months of the company's $1.76 billion in annual preferred-stock dividends and debt interest, giving it room to service obligations through a bitcoin downturn.

title: Trump's crypto riches loom over Clarity Act talks to ban conflicts for U.S. officials
blurb: Senate Democrats have zeroed in on President Trump's disclosed $1.4B in crypto-related gains as they negotiate the conflict-of-interest provision in the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act. The ethics language remains unresolved with a new draft expected within days, one of the final obstacles to advancing the market structure bill to a Senate floor vote before the calendar runs out.

title: Bitcoin and Ethereum tweet volume falls to 12-month lows despite institutional crypto boom
blurb: Bitcoin and Ethereum tweet volume has fallen to 12-month lows, putting retail social chatter back at 2020 levels even as institutional participation climbs through ETFs and corporate treasuries. The divergence signals that the current cycle is being driven by allocators rather than retail speculation, with organic social engagement no longer tracking price or on-chain activity.

title: Wall Street transfer agents lobby SEC, warning that third-party tokens pose risks to market integrity
blurb: The Securities Transfer Association, a trade group for Wall Street transfer agents, urged the SEC to give issuer-sponsored tokenized shares preferential treatment over third-party stock tokens, arguing only issuer-authorized tokens recorded in official shareholder registers should count as true tokenized equity. The group warned that synthetic and platform-issued tokens add credit, custody, and operational risks as firms race to build a multitrillion-dollar tokenized securities market.

title: Chinese prosecutors call for more proactive approach to investigating crypto money laundering
blurb: Chinese prosecutors urged a more proactive stance on investigating crypto-related money laundering, signaling tighter enforcement in a country that already bans domestic trading and services. The push suggests authorities view crypto channels as a growing conduit for illicit fund flows, adding regulatory pressure for exchanges and OTC desks that serve mainland users from offshore.

title: Stablecoin FX priced below interbank rates in Q2, with routing now the biggest cost lever: Borderless
blurb: Stablecoin payments cleared 3.2 bps below interbank FX rates across 260 corridors in Q2, according to Borderless.xyz's benchmark, flipping stablecoin rails from a premium to a discount against traditional settlement. With pricing now below the interbank line, Borderless identifies corridor routing rather than spread as the largest remaining cost lever for cross-border payment providers.

title: Cap Cuts Its 'Stabledrop' Airdrop to $4.2M from $12M as Backlash Mounts
blurb: Cap, a Franklin Templeton-backed stablecoin protocol, cut its promised "Stabledrop" reward to $4.2M from the roughly $12M pledged in February, after a downsized token sale cleared at a $75M floor instead of the targeted $250M valuation. The reversal renews scrutiny of discretionary airdrop programs and tests user trust for a protocol holding about $230M in TVL as it expands into on-chain credit.

title: Sablier Labs Enters Maintenance Mode, Halts Development
blurb: Sablier Labs halted active product development and entered maintenance mode until June 2028, with CEO Paul Berg citing a sharp Q1 2026 drop in usage and revenue and concluding there is no venture-scale business in onchain money streaming. Existing streams and vesting plans remain live on the permissionless contracts, and Sablier immediately converted its EVM code from BSL 1.1 to GPL so the community can fork and redeploy.

title: Coinbase CEO Says Base's Content Coins 'Didn't Work'
