blurb: Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong said Base's yearlong push into creator "content coins" failed, writing on X that the network "messed up" and pivoted early this year toward trading, payments, and AI agents. ZORA, the token behind Base's creator-coin tooling, has lost about 95% from its August high and now carries a $30M market cap, down from roughly $800M at the frenzy's peak.

title: Uniswap Auctions Go Live on Robinhood Chain
blurb: Uniswap deployed its Continuous Clearing Auctions and no-code Auctions tool to Robinhood Chain, letting teams run fully onchain token sales that discover price over multiple blocks before seeding a Uniswap v4 pool. Robinhood Chain auctions surface directly in the Uniswap Web App for launching, browsing, bidding, and claiming, extending last month's no-code tool to the network that went live in early July.

title: Strategy Sells $467M in MSTR Shares, Bitcoin Stack Steady
blurb: Strategy sold $466.7M of MSTR common stock between July 6 and July 12, raising its USD reserve to $3B while holding its bitcoin stack flat at 843,775 BTC, per a Form 8-K filed July 13. The cash is earmarked for preferred-stock dividends and debt interest, now covering more than 20 months of those obligations, and the company added no new BTC.

title: Trump Urges Senate to Pass Clarity Act for Lindsey Graham
blurb: Trump urged the Senate to pass the Clarity Act crypto market structure bill in honor of Senate Banking chair Lindsey Graham, who died July 11, framing the vote as a race against China. The bill, which would split token oversight between the SEC and CFTC, targets floor action the week of July 20 and needs 60 votes ahead of the August 7 recess.

title: Trump calls for Congress to pass Clarity Act crypto bill to honor Lindsey Graham
blurb: Trump called on the Senate to pass the Clarity Act crypto market-structure bill, framing the vote as a tribute to the late Sen. Lindsey Graham and a hedge against Chinese dominance in digital assets. The bill cleared the Senate Banking Committee 15-9 in May but faces Democratic demands for stricter official-conduct guardrails, and Graham's death narrows the Republican majority to 52-47.

title: Mizuho says Circle bank approval doesn't solve USDC growth, stablecoin competition risks
blurb: Mizuho reiterated its neutral rating on Circle, arguing that the OCC's final approval for a national trust bank is a positive step but doesn't fix slowing USDC growth, whose supply has fallen roughly $7B from its March peak to about $74B. The bank also flagged Open USD, a GENIUS Act-compliant stablecoin backed by 140+ firms including Mastercard and Stripe, as a rising competitive threat.

title: Robinhood built a blockchain for tokenized stocks. Memecoins took over
blurb: Robinhood Chain, the Ethereum L2 the trading app launched July 1 to host tokenized stocks, has pulled in roughly $312M in total value locked and 3.6M daily transactions, briefly overtaking Base in transaction count. Tokenized real-world assets account for just $12.8M of that activity, with memecoins like CASHCAT and stablecoins dominating volume, echoing Base's own speculation-first start in 2023.

title: Bank of Thailand audits high-volume stablecoin trades to crack down on illicit finance: report
blurb: The Bank of Thailand and SEC have reportedly begun deploying data analytics tools to audit high-volume stablecoin trades, focusing on USDT flows tied to illicit finance. The scrutiny signals tighter oversight of Thailand's stablecoin market, where USDT dominates on-chain settlement and cross-border transfers that regulators have struggled to trace.

title: Profit-taking, MidEast hostilities drag crypto lower after bullish week
blurb: Bitcoin fell about 1% to roughly $63,100 on Monday as reignited Iran-U.S. tensions over the Strait of Hormuz pulled risk assets lower, with South Korea's Kospi down 9.2% and $253M in leveraged crypto positions liquidated over 24 hours. Altcoins led the drop, with LIT sliding 8% in its first major pullback after a 200% two-month rally, though derivatives positioning stayed measured with open interest steady at $17B.

title: DOJ to Drop Charges Against $722M BitClub Network Fraud Mastermind
blurb: The DOJ is preparing to drop charges with prejudice against Matthew Goettsche, the alleged mastermind behind BitClub Network, a crypto mining scheme prosecutors said defrauded investors of $722M. The department cited the case's seven-year timeline and victim-recovery efforts, denied that defense pressure influenced the move, and is still negotiating final terms before dismissal after three co-defendants pleaded guilty.

title: Strategy sells $467 million in MSTR shares, makes no bitcoin purchases as USD reserve hits $3 billion
blurb: Strategy sold $467 million in MSTR shares and made no bitcoin purchases last week, growing its USD reserve to $3 billion while holdings stayed flat at 843,775 BTC. The pause marks a shift from the firm's continuous accumulation strategy, with its stack now worth roughly $53 billion and representing about 4% of bitcoin's 21 million supply cap.

title: Solo bitcoin miner makes $200,000 using $150 equipment
blurb: A solo miner running a $60 to $150 Bitaxe device for eight hours found Bitcoin block 957,382 on Public Pool, earning 3.1382 BTC worth about $200,000 at an average hash rate near 1 terahash per second. Solo mining has climbed to 24 blocks over the past 12 months, up 41% year over year for a combined 75.44 BTC in payouts.

title: Hedera Network Hit for $5.25M in Apparent Exploit
blurb: Hedera's network was hit for roughly $5.25M in an apparent exploit, with the attacker draining funds before the incident was flagged on-chain. The loss adds to a run of DeFi and bridge exploits this year and puts renewed scrutiny on the security of smart-contract deployments across the ecosystem.

title: Gondor v1 to let users borrow against their entire Polymarket portfolio
blurb: Gondor announced v1 of its Polymarket margin product, letting users cross-margin their entire prediction-market portfolio to borrow against combined positions and buy more shares, with a public launch expected in September. The cross-margin model replaces the beta's isolated per-position borrowing, which Gondor said exposed lenders to gap risk and had to be capped to a few liquid markets.

title: Robinhood Chain volume is mostly memecoin-driven
blurb: Most of the transaction volume on Robinhood Chain is coming from memecoin trading rather than the payments, tokenized-equity, or DeFi activity the L2 was positioned around at launch. The concentration mirrors early Base and Solana usage patterns, where speculative tokens drive initial throughput before broader applications establish measurable share.

title: South Korea maintains blockchain economy push as AI takes center stage
blurb: South Korea's Ministry of Finance and Economy laid out an H2 2026 plan to enact the Digital Asset Basic Act, establish a legal framework for won-pegged stablecoins, and revise the Capital Markets Act to enable the country's first spot crypto ETFs. The plan also pairs a 2027 government-bond tokenization pilot with an institutional CBDC project, advancing digital-asset infrastructure even as AI dominates the broader agenda.

title: U.S. government moves $288 million in seized bitcoin, ether to Coinbase Prime
blurb: U.S. government wallets moved roughly $288 million in seized bitcoin and ether to Coinbase Prime on Monday, with the ether sent directly and the bitcoin routed through fresh intermediary wallets, per Arkham data. The transfers appear to conflict with Trump's March 2025 executive order directing seized bitcoin into a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve and barring its sale, though they cover only a fraction of the government's ~$20.65 billion in crypto holdings.

title: Resurgent U.S.-Iran hostilities send bitcoin lower even as ETF flows show demand
blurb: Bitcoin fell more than 1% to around $63,000 as renewed U.S.-Iran airstrikes over the Strait of Hormuz pushed Brent crude above $79 and triggered broad risk-off selling. Spot bitcoin and ether ETFs broke eight-week outflow streaks over the same period, with this week's CPI and PPI prints set to shape rate expectations and the crypto reaction.

title: Revolut to launch US bank in 2027, says US boss
blurb: Revolut plans to launch a standalone US bank in 2027, headquartered in Stamford, Connecticut, pending approval of the OCC and FDIC banking licence it applied for in March. A charter would let the $75B neobank drop its partner-bank arrangements and earn credit interchange directly, offering FDIC-insured deposits, credit cards, and crypto access to its one million-plus US retail customers.

title: Deutsche Bank and World Bank Team Up for Trade Finance Platform
blurb: The World Bank and Deutsche Bank launched a €1B trade finance platform aimed at frontier and emerging markets, sharing risk to fund cross-border trade that global banks have largely retreated from. The tie-up pairs the World Bank's development mandate and guarantees with Deutsche Bank's correspondent-banking reach, targeting the persistent trade finance gap that leaves emerging-market importers and exporters underserved by traditional lenders.

title: Block reaches $45m Cash App fraud probe settlement with US states
blurb: Block agreed to pay $45M to settle a multistate probe into Cash App's anti-money-laundering and fraud controls, resolving allegations that weak KYC/AML systems let illicit accounts operate on the platform. The settlement follows Block's earlier $80M penalty from state regulators and a $175M CFPB order over Cash App fraud handling, keeping compliance costs a recurring drag on its consumer-payments unit.

title: Klarna Plans New Buy-Now-Pay-Later SRT as It Targets US Growth
blurb: Klarna is planning a significant risk transfer deal to offload credit risk on a portfolio of its buy-now-pay-later loans, freeing up capital to fund accelerated US and international expansion. The SRT structure lets Klarna keep originating BNPL volume without holding the full loss exposure on its balance sheet, a capital-efficiency move that matters as it scales lending in a competitive US market.

title: Sony secures conditional approval to set up US stablecoin trust bank
blurb: Sony Bank received preliminary conditional OCC approval to form Connectia Trust, a New York-based national trust bank capitalized with $40M to issue and manage dollar-denominated stablecoins. Operations are on hold pending final OCC sign-off under the GENIUS Act, and Sony enters a crowded field where Bridge, Paxos, and Circle already hold conditional charters and USDT and USDC control roughly $250B of a $311B market.

title: Moneybox crowned Europe’s newest unicorn as it gears up to test London’s Pisces market
blurb: Moneybox reached a $1.1B valuation and joined Europe's unicorn ranks as it prepares a £45M secondary sale for long-tenured employees on LSEG's new Pisces-framework Private Securities Market. The move makes Moneybox one of the first fintechs to test Pisces, the UK's bid to keep homegrown startups liquid at home after Revolut, OakNorth, and others weighed listing abroad.

title: ECB Digital Euro Trial Enlists Firms from Revolut to UniCredit
blurb: The ECB named 36 payment service providers, including Revolut, UniCredit, and Deutsche Bank, to run trials of the digital euro across conditional payments, offline use, and other functions. The pilot moves the central bank digital currency closer to a rollout that would give European banks and fintechs a state-backed rail, pressuring existing card networks and private stablecoin efforts on the continent.

title: President Trump, White House press Senate to advance the Clarity Act while ethics fight looms
blurb: Trump and White House officials are pressing the Senate to advance the Clarity Act, which would establish the first federal framework for regulating the crypto industry. New bill text is expected this week and could reach a full Senate floor vote before returning to the House, with only a few legislative weeks left to move it.

title: SBI Holdings, Solana Foundation partner to build Japan-based onchain financial market
blurb: SBI Holdings and the Solana Foundation partnered to build a Japan-based onchain financial market, rebranding SBI R3 to SBI Solana Global as the joint venture's operating entity. The move deepens SBI's push into tokenized settlement and gives Solana a regulated onshore foothold in one of Asia's largest capital markets, where local licensing has kept most crypto rails at arm's length.

title: US banking groups urge Senate to strengthen stablecoin provisions in Clarity Act
blurb: US banking groups including the American Bankers Association urged the Senate to tighten the stablecoin provisions in the Clarity Act, warning that the current language could let stablecoins substitute for insured bank deposits. The groups argue this would accelerate deposit flight from community banks, draining the low-cost funding that supports local lending.

title: Robinhood Chain draws over $3 billion in weekly DEX volume to join top five chains: Bernstein
blurb: Robinhood Chain drew roughly $3.1 billion in decentralized exchange volume in its first week live, ranking among the top five chains by DEX activity, according to a Bernstein research note. Since the July 1 mainnet launch, more than 65,000 users hold $300 million in stablecoins and $13 million in tokenized stocks on the Arbitrum-based Layer 2, which pairs tokenized equities with Morpho lending and Lighter perpetuals.

title: Bolivia weighs adding Tether's USDT to its national payments system
blurb: Bolivia is evaluating a framework to integrate Tether's USDT into its national payments system as a regulated alternative to the boliviano and U.S. dollar, though the stablecoin would not carry legal-tender status. The move follows a 630% jump in crypto transaction volume after the central bank lifted restrictions in mid-2024, but any rollout hinges on stronger AML controls given Bolivia's spot on the FATF grey list.

title: BlackRock, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley join UK government's tokenization taskforce
blurb: The UK government launched a tokenization taskforce of 54 financial firms, including BlackRock, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley, HSBC, and UBS, that will spend the next year building live use cases starting with tokenized repo. Backed by the City of London Corporation and led by HM Treasury's Chris Woolard, the effort targets an RWA market BCG estimates could reach $88T by 2035.

title: UK lays out tokenized finance roadmap with projected £33 billion annual boost
blurb: The UK Treasury published a roadmap for tokenizing financial assets, projecting a £33 billion annual economic boost from moving securities, funds, and payments onto distributed ledgers. The plan hinges on modernizing settlement infrastructure to support real-time atomic settlement, putting pressure on banks and payment providers to upgrade rails that still clear on T+1 or slower.

title: Japan’s largest security token platform moves nearly $3 billion to Avalanche blockchain
blurb: Progmat, Japan's dominant security token platform spun out of Mitsubishi UFJ Trust, completed its migration of over ¥452 billion (about $2.7 billion) in tokenized assets from a permissioned Corda ledger to a dedicated public Avalanche Layer 1. The move opens assets that hold 64.6% of Japan's security token issuance value to EVM interoperability, with rights-transfer processing now 3 to 5 times faster and finality under two seconds.

title: Strategy pauses its Bitcoin buying spree to hoard a massive $3 billion cash cushion
blurb: Strategy stopped buying bitcoin on June 22 and sold 3,588 BTC for roughly $216 million to build its US dollar reserve to $3 billion, enough to cover about 20.4 months of preferred-stock dividends and debt interest. The pivot from accumulation to liquidity signals Strategy is bracing for a bitcoin bear market while protecting distributions on its Stretch (STRC) perpetual preferred securities.

title: SBI Holdings' blockchain initiative pivots to Solana for tokenization, stablecoin issuance
blurb: SBI Holdings pivoted its blockchain initiative from R3's permissioned Corda to Solana, rebranding the SBI R3 Japan venture as SBI Solana Global to handle stablecoin issuance and RWA tokenization. The joint venture, which counts SMFG and now the Solana Foundation among its backers, aims to link Japan's domestic market to global liquidity and build payment infrastructure for AI agents.

title: UK Treasury report on tokenization cites Ripple as convergence model
blurb: A UK Treasury-backed report from wholesale digital markets champion Chris Woolard names Ripple among the credentialed firms central to a 12-month plan to move tokenized repo, gilts and funds from sandbox to live markets. The report backs a hybrid model layering permissioned institutional networks over permissionless chains, while flagging chain reorganizations as an unresolved settlement-finality risk that traditional infrastructure does not face.

title: Moneybox to become unicorn in secondary share sale
blurb: Moneybox is preparing a secondary share sale of up to £45m that values the digital wealth platform at around £800m, a 45% jump since 2024 and its entry into unicorn territory. The sale runs through the London Stock Exchange's PISCES framework on its new Private Securities Market, with Crowdcube managing the fixed-price employee-to-investor process.

title: Nvidia Partner GMI Cloud Seeks $635 Million GPU-Backed Bank Loan
blurb: GMI Cloud, a US data center operator and Nvidia partner, is seeking a NT$20.45 billion ($635M) multi-tranche loan backed by customer GPU contracts, one of the first such financings in Asia. Collateralizing compute against signed usage contracts extends asset-backed lending into AI infrastructure, giving lenders a new underwriting model as regional demand for GPU capacity accelerates.

title: Trump's crypto riches loom over Clarity Act talks to ban conflicts for U.S. officials
blurb: Senate Democrats are pushing to expand the conflict-of-interest provision in the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act after President Trump disclosed roughly $1.4B in crypto-linked wealth, extending proposed ownership and disclosure bans to officials' family members. A new draft is expected within days as the Senate runs short on floor time, though negotiators have yet to settle the ethics language holding up the market structure bill.

title: Stablecoin FX priced below interbank rates in Q2, with routing now the biggest cost lever: Borderless
blurb: Stablecoin payments cleared 3.2 bps below interbank FX rates across 260 corridors in Q2, according to Borderless.xyz's benchmark, meaning stablecoin rails now beat traditional settlement on raw price. With the currency spread erased, routing between on- and off-ramp partners has become the largest remaining cost, shifting the competitive edge toward whoever optimizes corridor liquidity rather than whoever quotes the tightest FX.

title: Wall Street transfer agents lobby SEC, warning that third-party tokens pose risks to market integrity
blurb: The Securities Transfer Association, a trade group representing transfer agents and their Wall Street members, asked the SEC to give issuer-sponsored tokenized shares preferential treatment over third-party stock tokens, arguing only issuer-authorized tokens recorded in official shareholder registers count as real tokenized stock. The group warned that synthetic and platform-issued tokens saddle holders with credit, custody, and operational risks as firms race to build a multitrillion-dollar tokenized securities market.

title: Wall Street banks tighten rules on employee prediction-market bets
blurb: Goldman Sachs barred employees from trading on finance- and politics-related prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket, and other Wall Street banks are moving to tighten similar policies. The clampdown reflects growing compliance concern that CFTC-regulated event contracts on rates, elections, and economic data give bank staff a new venue for insider-adjacent trading that existing personal-trading rules never anticipated.

title: Moonfare Assets Top €4 Billion as PE Platform Draws Rich Clients
blurb: Moonfare's assets under management topped €4B ($4.6B), driven by continued inflows from wealthy individuals and family offices onto its private markets investing platform. The milestone underscores how digital platforms are pushing private equity access downstream to accredited retail and high-net-worth clients, a segment fund managers increasingly court as institutional fundraising slows.

title: Mizuho says Circle bank approval doesn't solve USDC growth, stablecoin competition risks
blurb: Mizuho reiterated its neutral rating on Circle, arguing that final OCC approval for the First National Digital Currency Bank charter is a positive milestone but doesn't fix the fundamentals dragging on the stock. The bank flagged USDC's roughly $7B supply contraction since March, now near $74B, and rising competition from Open USD, a GENIUS Act-compliant stablecoin backed by 140-plus firms including Mastercard and Stripe.

title: Robinhood built a blockchain for tokenized stocks. Memecoins took over
blurb: Robinhood Chain, the Ethereum layer-2 the trading app launched July 1 to host tokenized stocks, has drawn roughly $312M in total value locked and 3.6M daily transactions, briefly overtaking Coinbase's Base in transaction count. Tokenized real-world assets account for just $12.8M of that activity, with memecoins like CASHCAT and stablecoins driving the volume, leaving the chain's equity-tokenization thesis unproven.

title: Fintechs face an ‘existential threat’ that excludes them from the CGT fix for startups
blurb: FinTech Australia told Treasury that almost all fintech startups would be ineligible for the proposed Innovative Business CGT Concession, the carve-out meant to shield startups from May's budget changes that replace the 50% CGT discount with cost-base indexation and a 30% minimum tax from July 2027. The industry body calls the exclusion an existential threat to a sector contributing $13.6B in direct economic value.

title: Nopan bags €7.2M: The ex-Netflix duo on a mission to close the reliability gap in Europe’s bank and wallet payments
blurb: Nopan raised €7.2M, led by Newion with backing from Crane and Seedcamp, to close the reliability gap between account and wallet payments and cards across Europe. Rather than building new rails, the Amsterdam-based startup optimizes routing, bank connectivity, and performance for methods merchants already run like Wero, where checkout failures and abandonment still trail card payments.

title: Netflix alumni-founded Nopan reaches €7.2 million in funding to scale account and wallet payments across Europe
blurb: Nopan, an Amsterdam-based payments platform founded by former Netflix payments leaders, raised €7.2M led by Newion, with Crane, Seedcamp, and angel investors participating. The startup builds an optimization layer for account and wallet payment methods across Europe, targeting the routing, bank connectivity, and reliability gaps that keep alternatives like Wero from matching card-level conversion for merchants and PSPs.

title: Former Netflix payments team raises €7.2M to date for payment performance platform Nopan
blurb: Nopan raised €7.2M to date, including a new round led by Newion with follow-on from Crane and Seedcamp, to build performance infrastructure for account-to-account and wallet payments. Founded by former Netflix payments leaders Konstantin Surkov and Nick Ryabov, the platform gives merchants and PSPs an optimization layer to lift conversion and cut costs on non-card rails like Wero as they scale across Europe.

title: Contractors wait months to get paid: Prolo raises £4.2M to fix that with AI and credit
blurb: Prolo raised £4.2M led by Triple Point Ventures to give SME contractors AI procurement and 90-day credit.

title: Elliott builds stake in CCC as software firm explores sale
blurb: Elliott Management has built a stake in CCC Intelligent Solutions, the insurance-claims software provider, as the company explores a potential sale. The activist involvement signals pressure on CCC to pursue strategic options, and a deal would put one of the largest platforms connecting insurers, repair shops, and lenders in the auto-claims workflow into new ownership.

title: Google: Quantum tech to break Bitcoin's cryptography by 2029
blurb: Google published March 2026 research concluding that quantum computers capable of breaking the elliptic-curve cryptography securing Bitcoin and other blockchains could arrive as early as 2029, roughly a decade ahead of prior estimates. Google is now working with the Ethereum Foundation and Coinbase to push the industry toward quantum-resistant signatures, a migration that threatens the roughly $2tn in assets tied to today's key schemes.
